registry.notes: All d01-d15 + capture_d_features are explicitly registered in the parent-level RELEASES dict.

inheritance.verdict: all chains valid, MRO consistent, no diamond issues
inheritance.notes: d14 inherits from D11Release (not DriveVariant directly), which correctly stacks the regime gate (d11) + close-position gate (via DriveVariant lever). d14 does NOT override build_candidates; it inherits D11Release.build_candidates which checks SPY<50d then delegates to DriveVariant.build_candidates which applies max_first_close_pos=0.9.

gates: NO filter change — uncapped target in build_signal
NO filter change — exit_cutoff moved to 15:55

d05.notes: Pre-registered control; backlog confirms ~no-op (0 gaps >40% in d-family)

gates: require_above_vwap=True (runtime gate in simulator, not build_candidates)
d06.notes: Needed engine change to simulate_long_breakout to compute session VWAP at fill time

d09.notes: Uses daily_atr_14 from existing daily context (daily_lookback_days=40 default sufficient for 14-day ATR)

d10.notes: Paired with d09. Same data path as d09.

gates: SPY prior close < 50-day SMA (whole-day gate, zero candidates if false)
d11.notes: Overrides build_candidates: checks _spy_below_sma first, returns [] if not below. Then delegates to super (DriveVariant). Adds spy_below_50d_sma:True to features.

gates: per-sector ETF < 50-day SMA (per-candidate), fallback to SPY < 50d if unmapped
d12.notes: Overrides build_candidates: delegates to super first, then applies per-sector gate with SPY fallback. Uses _below_50d_sma helper. Loads sector_map.yaml via _load_sector_map classmethod.

gates: max_first_close_pos=0.9 (reject first bars closing in top decile)
d13.notes: Standalone geometry gate, NO regime gate. Backlog: survives screen but 2026H1-carried; ex-2026H1 = -17.8R.

d14.notes: Inherits D11Release.build_candidates (SPY regime gate) + sets max_first_close_pos=0.9 on class. Gate order: whole-day SPY filter first, then per-candidate close-position via DriveVariant.build_candidates. Backlog: SURVIVED screen (+48.7R), KILLED on broad eval (2022 -6.8R, 2023 -16.2R).

additional_declared: historical_5m_lookback_days for RV baseline (30d matches capture run)
d15.notes: Feature-search winner combo. KILLED on sealed 2025 OOS (-34.1R ledger, -30.1R engine cross-check). Kept as record + engine-fidelity cross-check.

M2_L2_opening_relative_volume_timezone.detail: Line 46: if timezone-naive, between_time applied to naive index. Works with naive data but relies on bars being in NY time already — fragile if provider returns UTC or other tz. Handler is defensive (tz_convert or keep naive) but the naive path is unguaranteed.

L3_build_candidates_mutates_features_in_.detail: Lines 99-100 now do `f = dict(c.features); c.features = f` — a clone before mutation. Confirmed. Prior concern about line 109-129 in-place mutation is addressed.

L6_historical_5m_lookback_days_leaf_only.detail: historical_5m_lookback_days set only on leaf classes that need it: d02 (14), d15 (30), capture.py (30). All others inherit default=0 from StrategyRelease base. This is the correct pattern.

M16_d09_d10_undocumented_ATR_data_gate.detail: d09/d10 use daily_atr_14 which reads from context.daily — already hydrated by d01's requirements (daily_lookback_days=40 default). No additional data declaration needed. The ATR filter is a computation on existing data, not a new data requirement.

L17_variants_py_docstring_stale.detail: variants.py module docstring line 1 says 'Parametrized base for the d02–d04' but the class now serves d02-d10, d11, d12, d13, d15 (11 releases). Docstring and numbered-list body (lines 7-19) only describe d02/d03/d04.

variants_py.claim: Parametrized base for the d02-d04 post-gap-opening-drive variants
variants_py.actual: Serves d02-d10, d11, d12, d13, d15 (11 releases)
variants_py.verdict: STALE — docstring describes only d02/d03/d04, class now used by 11 releases

claims.d02: add premarket volume and gap-hold filters
claims.d04+: add common market-regime and execution-quality filters

d01_next_intended.verdict: STALE — d01's 'Next intended releases' section describes different releases than what was actually built

d11_next_intended.verdict: STALE — d11 predicted different follow-on releases

d13_docstring.verdict: ACCURATE — describes its own gate correctly, no 'Next intended' mismatches beyond what d14 addresses

d15_docstring.verdict: ACCURATE — correctly describes killed status, feature-search provenance, and engine cross-check purpose

filter_until_zero_assessment.concern: Stacking sparsifying gates (regime + geometry) could collapse trade counts

evidence.d11_bull_stretches: SPY>50d days produce ZERO candidates (by design)

filter_until_zero_assessment.verdict: PASS — trade counts remain above the >20/quarter floor on broad eval. However, d11/d12/d14 are regime-gated (only fire in weak markets) so trade counts are sparser than ungated releases. d14 was killed at stage-2 broad eval for negative sumR, not for thin trades.

d05_split_guard.triggered_in_d05: Backlog confirms ZERO trades tripped this guard (diagnostic found 0 gaps >40% in d01's 1343 trades)
d05_split_guard.verdict: ~no-op for the d-family — the guard exists but never fires on this universe

capture_py_split_guard.purpose: Data-integrity gate on the capture ledger (wider window to catch split artifacts in training data)
capture_py_split_guard.verdict: Different use case from d05 — capture uses extended 252-day lookback for max recall on data-integrity events

zero_range_bar.behavior: Kept (not filtered). Comment says 'degenerate zero-range bars are not the target — keep'
zero_range_bar.verdict: INTENTIONAL — avoids divide-by-zero, passes degenerate bars through

negative_close_pos.condition: first_close < first_low (theoretically impossible for valid candle)
negative_close_pos.behavior: Would produce negative close_pos < 0.9, candidate kept
negative_close_pos.verdict: HARMLESS — guardrail against corrupt data, passes through rather than filtering

close_pos_gt_1.verdict: HARMLESS — corrupt data would be filtered, correct candles with close==high produce 1.0 > 0.9, correctly filtered

all_inputs_zero.verdict: HARMLESS — degenerate data not falsely filtered

max_first_close_pos_edge_cases.overall: Edge cases handled defensively. The threshold 0.9 correctly identifies bars closing at/near high. Zero-range bars passed through intentionally.

d15_min_gap_pct_override.resolution: d15's min_gap_pct=3.0 overrides D01Release's 1.0. D01Release.build_candidates uses self.min_gap_pct which resolves to 3.0 for d15 instances. Combined with min_rv=1.5 from DriveVariant filter.
d15_min_gap_pct_override.verdict: CORRECT — gap floor properly raised from 1% to 3% for d15

d11.sma_logic: SPY prior close < trailing 50-day SMA of prior closes (no look-ahead, context.spy_daily is strictly before trade_date)

d12.sector_fallback: per-sector ETF < 50d SMA, falls back to SPY < 50d for unmapped tickers or short ETF history

d15.parent_filter: min_gap_pct=3.0 in D01Release.build_candidates, min_rv=1.5 in DriveVariant.build_candidates, historical_5m_lookback_days=30

overall_verdict.summary: All 15 releases (d01-d15) exist, are registered, and have valid inheritance. No broken MRO, no missing data declarations that would cause runtime errors. Immutable release files intact.

opening_relative_volume timezone-naive path (variants.py:46) — works with NY-naive bars but fragile
Family is effectively retired: d15 killed on OOS, d14 killed on broad eval, no surviving release has passed stage-2 broad eval

M16 ATR data gate is not actually an issue — daily context already loaded
L6 historical_5m_lookback_days correctly on leaf classes only
All filter-until-zero thresholds respected (trade counts healthy on broad eval)
split_guard correctly implemented (even if ~no-op for d-family)

d14 correctly stacks d11 regime gate + d13 geometry gate via inheritance chain
All releases registered in parent __init__.py RELEASES dict
